summary: The guiding cusps involved in occlusion include the maxillary buccal cusp and mandibular lingual cusp, which provide dynamic guidance during lateral jaw movements through controlled interaction at embrasure areas. These interactions occur within functional embouchure spaces, emphasizing a biomechanical relationship over static dental contacts or groove-based structures.
